title: lobelia ceratophylla var. glabrata
intro: Lobelia ceratophylla var. glabrata, also known as smooth Indian tobacco or glabrous lobelia, is a variety of the Lobelia ceratophylla plant, a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family, Campanulaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of lobeline, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The health benefits of Lobelia ceratophylla var. glabrata include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion and reduce inflammation, as well as its potential to improve circulation and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, with caution advised due to its potential toxicity and the need for proper dosing.
